notes: Flagship NE Iowa river; smallmouth and browns.
Yellow River State Forest reaches; wild browns.

Beaver Creek Valley State Park reach. Flow proxy: South Fork Root River near Houston (USGS 05385500) — spring-influenced, ~12 mi N. Switched from the formerly-used lower-Root-mainstem 05385000 because that gauge is freestone and over-reads storm percentiles for spring creeks like Beaver.

Tight spring creek south of Winona. Flow proxy: NOAA North Fork Whitewater near Elba (EBWM5) — same Winona County bluff country, same spring-fed hydrology. Switched from the formerly-used Trempealeau at Dodge (DDGW3) because Trempealeau is a freestone WI mainstem with very different recession behavior.; flow percentile via proxy gauge: Rush Creek near Rushford (24km, similar small Mississippi-tributary spring creek)

Heavily-fished MN spring creek just south of Red Wing. Proxy: NOAA Wells Creek near Frontenac, ~11 mi NE (same Red Wing bluff-country drainage).; flow percentile via proxy gauge: Kinnickinnic near River Falls (42km, nearest USGS spring-creek-class gauge)

Wild brown stronghold; heavily spring-influenced. NOAA gauge is on this reach (USGS version was decommissioned in 1993).; flow percentile via proxy gauge: Root R at Pilot Mound (34km, similar regional spring-creek class)

Houston County spring creek; Class I throughout. Pine Cr is a small Mississippi-tributary spring creek with no gauge of its own. Flow proxy: South Fork Root River near Houston (USGS 05385500) — chosen over the (formerly used) lower-Root-mainstem 05385000 because S Fk Root is spring-influenced and similar small-creek hydrology. ~12 mi N, same Houston County bluff country.
Lower Root; marginal summer temps, spring and fall prime.
Main stem Root at Lanesboro; gauge is 3 mi downstream on the same reach.
Classic Driftless spring creek; Trichos and Sulphurs. Gauge reports stage only (no discharge).
Class I wild browns; spring-fed headwaters.

Popular sight-fishing water near Altura. Proxy: NOAA Middle Fork Whitewater at Whitewater State Park Group Camp, ~4 mi S (sibling fork of same Whitewater drainage).; flow percentile via proxy gauge: Rush Creek near Rushford (31km, similar regional spring-creek class)

Spring creek with DNR easements; wild browns. Proxy: NOAA Middle Branch Root at Fillmore, ~10 mi SW (same Middle Branch Root drainage).; flow percentile via proxy gauge: Root R at Pilot Mound (12km, same Root River watershed)

Whitewater River at Beaver; Whitewater State Park reach. Proxy: NOAA North Fork Whitewater near Elba, ~5 mi upstream (same drainage).; flow percentile via proxy gauge: Rush Creek near Rushford (40km, similar regional spring-creek class)

SE MN spring creek. Proxy: NOAA Waterloo Creek near Dorchester, ~8 mi S (small-stream proxy, similar class).; flow percentile via proxy gauge: South Fork Root near Houston (23km, spring-influenced regional proxy)
Apple River at Park in Somerset; mixed wild and stocked.

Bootstrapped from WI DNR trout-regulation geometry (1 mapped segments). Flow proxy: READS CREEK AT RILEY ROAD NEAR READSTOWN, WI (USGS 05409270); water temperature remains reach-estimated rather than inherited from the proxy watershed.
Bootstrapped from WI DNR trout-regulation geometry (1 mapped segments) and paired to local USGS gauge 05371920.

Bootstrapped from WI DNR trout-regulation geometry (1 mapped segments). Flow proxy: COON CREEK AT COON VALLEY, WI (USGS 05386500); water temperature remains reach-estimated rather than inherited from the proxy watershed.
Bootstrapped from WI DNR trout-regulation geometry (2 mapped segments) and paired to local USGS gauge 05386500.
Lower Kickapoo near La Farge; warm-water/marginal trout.
Upper Kickapoo; Class II trout. USGS gauge 05407468 (Kickapoo R @ ST HWY 131 at Ontario) is on this reach — active, reports flow + stage.
Bootstrapped from WI DNR trout-regulation geometry (1 mapped segments) and paired to local USGS gauge 05410490.
Popular limestone spring creek; heavy BWO and Sulphur hatches.
Bootstrapped from WI DNR trout-regulation geometry (1 mapped segments) and paired to local USGS gauge 05382325.

Bootstrapped from WI DNR trout-regulation geometry (1 mapped segments). Flow proxy: NORTH FORK BAD AXE RIVER NEAR GENOA, WI (USGS 05387100); water temperature remains reach-estimated rather than inherited from the proxy watershed.
Bootstrapped from WI DNR trout-regulation geometry (1 mapped segments) and paired to local USGS gauge 05387100.
Bootstrapped from WI DNR trout-regulation geometry (2 mapped segments) and paired to local USGS gauge 05371050.
Bootstrapped from WI DNR trout-regulation geometry (1 mapped segments) and paired to local USGS gauge 05409270.
Bootstrapped from WI DNR trout-regulation geometry (1 mapped segments) and paired to local USGS gauge 05355322.

Class I wild brown trout; Kiap-TU-Wish restoration reaches. Rush's own USGS gauge 05355325 is inactive (verified 2026-04) and has no published daily statistics. Flow proxy: Kinnickinnic at River Falls (USGS 05342000) — chosen over the geographically-closer Eau Galle (05370000) because Kinni is a same-class spring-influenced limestone creek with similar drainage area (165 vs ~140 sqmi); Eau Galle is freestone with a much flashier flow distribution and would over-read storm percentiles for Rush. The structural water-temp guard still forces Mohseni for thermal regime.

Willow River at Willow River State Park near Burkhardt. Proxy: Apple River at Somerset, ~9 mi N (same St. Croix drainage; Willow's own gauge was discontinued in Jan 2024).
